rationale: Live-action UGC of a guy in bed bragging about reaching "level 55" fundamentally misaligns with Flappy Dunk's hyper-casual, no-progression, reflex-arcade loop — there's no level system or narrative payoff to mimic, and faking one risks misleading-ad penalties. Visually, the muted blue/grey/black UGC palette and bedroom setting clash with the game's high-saturation magenta/beige cartoon style, and the puzzle-game framing in the reference hook signals the wrong genre to viewers. Audience overlap with casual mobile gamers exists, but the archetype is low-velocity (2 creatives, 1 advertiser) and would need a heavy rewrite around quick-reflex bragging or VS-mode trash talk to be worth testing.
